title: Citrusy Fluffy French Lentils Soup
description: A vibrant, Jain-friendly lentil soup infused with lemon and orange, featuring blanched green and red lentils for a fluffy texture.
instructions: Rinse the green and red lentils under cold water until the water runs clear.
Bring 2 cups of water to a rapid boil in a medium pot; add the lentils, simmer for 2 minutes to blanch, then drain and set aside.
Heat the olive oil in the same pot over medium heat; add the chopped celery and sauté for 2 minutes until softened.
Stir in the cumin, coriander, and turmeric; cook for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the blanched lentils back into the pot and stir for 1 minute to coat with spices.
Pour in the vegetable broth, bring to a rolling boil, then reduce heat to a gentle simmer; cover and cook for 20 minutes until the lentils are tender.
Add the lemon zest, orange zest, lemon juice, and orange juice; stir well and let simmer uncovered for 5 minutes to meld flavors.
Remove from heat and use an immersion blender to purée 3/4 of the soup until silky smooth, leaving a quarter still chunky for texture.
Return the soup to the pot, gently reheat, and adjust seasoning if needed before serving.
Garnish each bowl with a sprinkle of fresh parsley and serve hot.
